name: Aekvator kaffe
comment:
Espresso Equador 250gr. Bought in store in Nyborg. 
I think it was old when I bought it, beans very dry and tasteless. 
I ended up using the beans in my plants to deter gnats. 

Was going to buy online but they only accept dankort or bank transfer, not even mobile pay. I really cannot be bothered.

name: Als Risteri
comment: Als espresso "full city roast" 220gr. Not a bad coffee by any means. I found that if I set the grinder to a 7 or 8 I got the most flavour without too much acidity. Any coarser and it turned watery. I definitely like this one the most with regular whole milk or skimmed milk. The oat milk is quite sweet and found it overpowered the mild flavour. Super quick service, they shipped the same day I ordered and I got it to my pakkeshop the day after.

name: Andersen & Maillard
comment: Lalesa Black Honey 250gr. This one was a delight. I broke my always purchase coffee from a new roaster rule just to get another bag of this. The coffee made me think of cherries, and as weird as that sounds, I cannot get enough of it. I kept the grinder a little coarser here because the beans were very glossy (oily?) and a finer setting just created over extracted tar. I loved everything about this coffee and found it perfect with the sweet thick oat milk. Loved this. Excellent quick service. Not the cheapest but 100% worth it.

name: April Coffee Roasters
comment: I did not venture into trying the 600kr for 125gr. But I went for a 250gr decaf and a 250gr House blend keneya. Both terrific. It is not too easy to find good decaf and this made for a very full coffee without the slightly watery or even toxic taste that I often get from decaf. This one did very well with the fatter whole milk and the Oatly. I personally thought the skimmed milk did not do it justice. The Kenya was ok. Not a bad coffee not a fantastic one either. I kept it to a grind setting 8 usually after trying even down to 6. Stellar fast service.

name: Berry & Bean
comment: Ethiopia Sidamo 250gr

name: Buchwalds
comment: Buchwald's Espresso - 250g. I am dying to visit Bornholm one day, though it was especially interesting to try out a roastery from this more distant of the Danish Islands. Somewhat mild espresso that I mostly used in skimmed milk and with a finer grinding setting. The oat milk hid the coffee flavour instead of bringing it out. This one was a little tricky for me. I used a much finer setting than I normally do for any espresso to get the most flavour out. But once I got there I really enjoyed it. Loved the design. Flawless checkout and payment service although GLS decided to hold on to this for an extra couple of days so it took a little longer than most to get the coffee.

name: Clever Coffee
comment: Kindeng - Papua Ny Guinea, 250gr. Insanely good. This was a clear favourite from the start. It was hard to screw this one up. I loved it with Oatly, whole and skimmed milk and actually just found various grinding settings to bring out interest more than possible ruin the coffee. Stellar service. Had a brewed cup the day after I purchased online. Lovely coffee.

name: Coffee Collective
comment: Subscription - have gotten I think 10 different kinds.

name: København Coffee Lab
comment: 3 bags   Espresso Tasting Pack

name: Emo Fabrik
comment:
Espresso Honduras Øko Specialty Coffee - Comsa - 250 g 
Peru Øko Specialty Coffee - 250 g

name: Europa Kaffe og Te
comment:
Goma – En sødmefuld nyhøstet kaffe fra Etiopien 250gr & Incahuasi 250gr. 
 Out of the two I bought I finished Goma in a matter of days. Loved the sweet fruity taste. Not finicky at all to get the full coffee flavour. I kept the temp at 95°and grind at 7 or 8. Amazing coffee. Incahuasi I cant seem to get right. It's been a few months and I still have some left. The beans have dried out now and I cannot do this bag justice.  
 With certain coffeee my macine cannot seem to properly extract the full flavour, and this one always got that slight dirt flavour that comes with ove extraction. 
 No matter the grind, water amount and temp settings. I feel its one of those coffeees that need a more professional machine than mine to shine.
 Goma however worked wonderfully. I only had it with Oatmilk from Oatly. THe grey package.

name: GarageRisteriet
comment: Økologisk Espresso Blend 250gr

name: Holy Bean
comment:
Costa Rica Tres milagros 250gr . The costa rica is fabulous
Etiopien Suke Quto  250gr
. I have repurhcased their Costa Rica Tres milagros a couple of times. Heavenly coffee. Sits just right with a grind setting of about 7 and any milk of choice. Not a finicky coffee by any means.  
 Etiopien Suke Quto never had that amazing aroma and flavour. Perhaps its the ugly duckling that would have shone better in anohter setting than what I offered. 
 Also , must admit, since I live in Fyen, I am a little biased towards coffee roasted here.  
 I will buy from this roastery again but I still have more than 20 to try.

name: Impact Roasters
comment:
Coffee Beans - MAZAA - Guji 250gr. Sweet and juicy with notes of honey and spice. Naturally processed and sun-dried on raised beds in the forested hills of Guji. Smooth and medium-bodied with a deep, vibrant character. I could select which kind of roast I wanted here. Thats is the only roastery I have shopped form that has this option. For the fun of it I select this same bean in light roast and medium roast. 
 Since starting on this coffee journey I am not that into dark roast anymhas this option. For the fun of it I select this same bean in light roast and medium roast. 
 Since starting on this coffee journey I am not that into dark roast anymore..

name: Just Coffee
comment:
Espresso Slowdown koffeinfri  250gr. Really good decaf. Ended up buying an extra kilo.
Note, I love their Ethiopian Natural and put trying new roasteries on hold for a while just to have that coffee for a month.  
 In fact I cheat and I always have a bag of their Ethiopian natural at hoome now. 
 If I get dissapointing roasts that my machine cannot work with, I comfort myself with a cup r two from this roast.  
 setting 7, 95°and just the factory default extraction time.  
 In a Pixar world,this coffee would keep machine from getting depressed if it tries with too many roasts that it does not do justice. It needs a coffee every now and then that hits the sweet spot everytime.

name: Kaffe Lars
comment: Ethiopia, Sidamo 500gr - not possible to buy smaller :(

name: Kaffe Værkstedet
comment: Espresso Bomben 225gr. Dark roast

name: Kaffeagenterne
comment:
Månedens Kaffe - Despresso Espresso Decaff, ristet 500gr. I really hate when I am forced to buy more than 250gr. I wanted to find a decaf from a roastery I havent tried before. Most of the roasteries do not have decaf so I went for this one anyway.
 I usually don't shop if there is no option for a smaller bag though. 
 These coffees are expensive and I dont want to end up with 500gr of pricy beans that may not suit me or my machine. However the price here was about the same as a 250gr in other places so I ordered a 500gr

name: Kaffevaerket
comment: They don't have an online store, Have to go to the coffee shop to buy their coffee.

name: Køge Kafferisteri
comment:
NICARAGUA Limited Edition  × 1
Mængde:
250 Gram. I had some luck with going all the way down to 6 on my grinder. Less than that , on fresh beans, and asphalt comes out and the machine sounds like its about to self-destruct. 
 This is a milder flavoured coffee that needs the higher temp and finer grind to flourishm but cheaper home models like mine have a hard time with that.  
 I managed to get a decent cup after prolonging the extraction time a little, allowing for a second of more water ( it really can turn the coffee from sour to delicious, a single second more ) , then raising the temperature to 95° and the grind setting to 6. Tried this coffee with full fat Oatmilk and full fat regular milk and there was very little difference. 
 This coffee did not shine in my home machine. I think it needs a grade above mine to truly flourish

name: LA CABRA
comment:
Jorge Vásquez 250gr . Abslutely delicious. Tried a few grind settings and found 7 to be the best for me. 8 and 6 worked well also even if the extracion got a little longer / shorter. The coffee did not suffer at all. This one I only tried with Oatly barista oatmilk, the full fat grey one and the newer lighter flavored white packaging. 
 Was sad when I finished that last of the beans.

name: Nygaards Kaffe
comment:
Etiopisk Sidamo Økologisk 250gr . Came vacuum packed with a little dark sugar candy. Perfect coffee, loved the silky texture and taste. This came with a surprise twist, the first few cups I got it tasted really overextracted and more like dirty water than coffee. 
 Decided to give my machine a complete rince, decalc and water overhaul.
 I then reset it to factory settings but upped the temp from the default 93°to 95°.  
 Maybe its the hard water here or maybe the machine is inaccurate and actually does not heat to 93° ( there is no good way for me to measure the accuracy, the machine certainly does not offer live metadata) but fresh beans on the default 93°will always give that underextracted dirt flavor. 
 THe only roasts that are drinkable at default are the really black french roasts. 
 Low and behold I got one the the most amazing, full flavoured silky espressos of my life. 
 Since this revelation I fanatically clean my machine all the time. And I never try a new coffee without giving the Sage a 30 minute power cleanicing cycle. 
 Trying out freshly roasted beans in a dirty limescaled coffee machine is a crime to espresso and the people that work so hard to deliver these precious beans to us. 
 Always keep the machine clean and if poking around with settings a lot, a factory reset every now and then.

name: Nørre Snede Kafferisteri
comment: Koffeinfri Peru - økologisk 200g. Monsooned Malabar INDIA 200G.

name: Original Coffee
comment:
Bought 250gr in café in København, as with store bought as opposed to bying directly from the roastery it can be a bit stale when you get it. 
 It was an ok coffee, I ended up treating it like the big LaVazza bags I used to buy from Fotex. Could never get anything more exiting than ok. Not bad but not great either. Did the standard 5-8 grind setting, 93-97° and left the extraction time to the factory default ( rare that I have to mess with that). 
 A bit like the IKEA of coffee. Does no wow beu does not offend either. 
 The latte made for me in the coffee hoouse was 20 times better than anything I tried. This makes me think that this coffee simply needs much better pressure and extraction possibilities than my machine offers.

name: Risteriet
comment: Daily espresso 250gr

name: Strandvejsristeriet
comment:
Espresso Velo
Espressoblend. This is a light roast with a mild flavor. To make this coffee justice you need to grind it finely and use a relatively high temperature on the espresso machine. 
 I made several attempts, raised the temp to 97° in the end ( its a Sage Barista and setting the temprerature is a mic of barious button combinations and guesswork ) and as fine as I could muster. But sadly I don't think my machine ca do it justice. 
 I either got a sour under-extracted flavor where not enough water makes it though the coffee, or when trying coarser grind and lower temps I got the dirt flavour of over extraction. 
 I am sure that in a proper machine that handles finer grinds than mine , this coffee is excellent.  But I just could not make it work for me

name: Svendborg Kafferisteri
comment: Decaf Arabica 250g. Bought in store, the coffee was pretty stale and old. Can't really compete with coffees directly from the roasteries that are fresh.

name: Te & Kaffe specialisten
comment: Kaffe med julet smag 225g. Bought in store. Not good for espresso even if store attendant assured it was fine for espresso. Got a watery cinnamon tasting sewer water out of this. Perfectly fine for french press. It's not possible to purchase over the website. Need to find in a store somewhere. Not sure I can be bothered.

name: Winthers Kaffe
comment: Can't see on the website where the roastery is. The shop is in Odense and they claim their coffee is freshly roasted.
